2606.28182.title: LLawCo: Learning Laws of Cooperation for Modeling Embodied Multi-Agent Behavior
2606.28182.paper_summary: Embodied agents operating in decentralized and partially observable environments have attracted growing attention in recent years. However, existing large language model (LLM)-based agents often exhibit behaviors that are misaligned with their partners or inconsistent with the environment state, leading to inefficient cooperation and poor task success. To address this challenge, we propose a novel framework, Learning Laws of Cooperation (LLawCo), that enables embodied agents to autonomously align with both their partners and task objectives. Our framework allows agents to reflect on past failures to extract misaligned behavioral patterns, which are used to derive high-level behavioral laws, such as "Talk when necessary" and "Wait for partner." These laws are explicitly incorporated into the agents' chains of thought via supervised fine-tuning, aligning their reasoning with task requirements and the behavior of other agents. To evaluate our approach, we introduce PARTNR-Dialog, a large-scale multi-agent communicative and cooperative planning benchmark built on the PARTNR environment. Experiments on existing tasks and our new benchmark demonstrate significant improvements in cooperative efficiency and task success rates. Across four backbone LLMs, our method achieves average success rate improvements of 4.5% on the PARTNR-Dialog benchmark and 6.8% on the TDW-MAT benchmark over state-of-the-art open-source communicative agent frameworks. See the LLawCo project page for details: https://www.merl.com/research/highlights/LLawCo
2606.28182.paper_summary_zh: 在去中心化和部分可觀察環境中運作的具身代理近年來受到越來越多的關注。<lb/>然而，現有基於大型語言模型（LLM）的代理經常表現出與其夥伴不一致或與環境狀態不符的行為，導致合作效率低下和任務成功率不佳。<lb/>為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了一個新穎的框架——合作法則學習（LLawCo），使具身代理能夠自主地與其夥伴和任務目標對齊。<lb/>我們的框架允許代理反思過去的失敗，以提取不一致的行為模式，這些模式用來推導高層次的行為法則，例如「必要時交談」和「等待夥伴」。<lb/>這些法則通過監督微調明確地納入代理的思考鏈中，使其推理與任務要求和其他代理的行為對齊。<lb/>為了評估我們的方法，我們引入了PARTNR-Dialog，一個基於PARTNR環境的大規模多代理交互和合作規劃基準。<lb/>在現有任務和我們的新基準上的實驗顯示，合作效率和任務成功率有顯著提高。<lb/>在四個主幹LLM上，我們的方法在PARTNR-Dialog基準上實現了平均成功率提高4.5%，在TDW-MAT基準上提高6.8%，超過了最先進的開源交互代理框架。<lb/>有關詳細信息，請參見LLawCo項目頁面：https://www.merl.com/research/highlights/LLawCo
2606.28182.author: Qinhong Zhou et.al.
2606.28182.authors: Qinhong Zhou, Chuang Gan, Anoop Cherian
